rulings.Case Title: DAVID ABRAMOVITZ ET AL VS. FARHAT BAJJALIYA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Joinder Of Tamara Tasoff As N Indispensible Party And Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2018 line 1 PLAINTIFFs DAVID ABRAMOVITZ, BLAIR BRIDGES, MYRIAM FALLON, ADRIENNE KNOLL, LAUREN LEDERMAN, and LANCE SHANNON's Motion To Compel Joinder Of Tamara Tasoff As N Indispensible Party And Defendant GRANTED, no opposition filed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 3H RENOVATION SERVICES, LLC ET AL VS. BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANTs FAY SERVICING, LLC, ANH PHAM, BSI FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC., and SKYGROUP REALTY, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised by the demurrer. Second Amended Complaint must state all facts and causes of action in concise and clear language. All amendments must be in bold font. Late-filed oversized opposition is stricken. CCP 1005, CCP 1013, CRC 3.1113(d). The Court notes that opposing party again failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B (See September 19, 2018 minute order). The Court also notes that while counsel for Plaintiff indicates that her address is in Beverly Hills, CA (See Opposition caption page), counsel declares under the penalty of perjury: "I am employed in the County of San Francisco, State of California." (See Proof of Service). No explanation for such discrepancy provided; explanation is required to be provided as the hearing. Hearing Required to address why OSC Re: Contempt should not be set for failure to comply with "meet and confer" requirements. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: REAL MANAGEMENT COMPANY VS. KEITH LAMONT MOORE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT KEITH MOORE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the request for general damages is necessarily duplicative of the request for daily damages, i.e. that the only general damages that may be recovered in this action are general damages. Request for damages per CCP 1166(5)(b) is sufficiently stated, i.e. Plaintiff sufficiently states circumstances of force or violence that may have accompanied the alleged forcible entry and detainer. = (501/CFH)
